mission.title: Install and Configure IBM Cloud Kubernetes Service (IKS)
mission.description: IBM Cloud Kubernetes Service (IKS) is a managed Kubernetes offering that simplifies the deployment and management of Kubernetes clusters on IBM Cloud. It is ideal for users who want to leverage Kubernetes without the overhead of managing the underlying infrastructure. Key trade-offs include reliance on IBM Cloud for management and potential costs associated with cloud resources.

steps.title: Install the IBM Cloud CLI
steps.description:
This step installs the IBM Cloud CLI, which is necessary for interacting with IBM Cloud services.

```bash

curl -sL https://ibm.biz/idt-installer | bash

```

> This command downloads and installs the IBM Cloud CLI.

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes cluster
steps.description:
This command creates a new Kubernetes cluster with the specified version, node count, and region.

```bash

ibmcloud ks cluster-create --name my-cluster --zone us-south-1 --num-workers 2 --kube-version 1.30

```

> Adjust the zone and node count as needed.

steps.title: Set up kubeconfig
steps.description:
This command configures your local kubeconfig to access the newly created cluster.

```bash

ibmcloud ks cluster-config --cluster my-cluster

```

> This command updates your kubeconfig file.

steps.title: Verify cluster nodes
steps.description:
This command checks the status of the nodes in your Kubernetes cluster to ensure they are running.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> You should see the nodes listed with a 'Ready' status.

steps.title: Post-install configuration
steps.description:
This step includes setting up autoscaling, monitoring, and RBAC for your cluster.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/IBM-Cloud/kube-samples/main/autoscaler.yaml

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/IBM-Cloud/kube-samples/main/monitoring.yaml

kubectl create clusterrolebinding cluster-admin-binding --clusterrole=cluster-admin --user=$(ibmcloud iam whoami)

```

> This command sets up the Horizontal Pod Autoscaler.
> This command sets up monitoring for your cluster.
> This command grants cluster-admin privileges.
commands: kubectl create clusterrolebinding cluster-admin-binding --clusterrole=cluster-admin --user=$(ibmcloud iam whoami)

uninstall.title: Delete the Kubernetes cluster
uninstall.description:
This command removes the Kubernetes cluster and all associated resources. Be aware that this will result in data loss.

```bash

ibmcloud ks cluster-delete --cluster my-cluster --force

```

> This command deletes the cluster without confirmation.

uninstall.title: Clean up orphaned resources
uninstall.description:
This step ensures that any remaining resources such as load balancers and PVCs are cleaned up.

```bash

ibmcloud resource service-instance-delete <service-instance-name> --force

```

> Replace <service-instance-name> with your actual service instance name.

upgrade.title: Upgrade the Kubernetes cluster
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up your data and configurations. After upgrading, verify the cluster status.

```bash

ibmcloud ks cluster-update --cluster my-cluster --kube-version 1.30

kubectl get nodes

```

> This command upgrades the cluster to the specified version.
> Verify that all nodes are in 'Ready' status after the upgrade.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'No resources found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the specified resource does not exist in the current namespace.

**Fix:**
Check the resource name and namespace, or use 'kubectl get all --all-namespaces' to see all resources.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to connect to the server'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This usually means that the kubeconfig is not set up correctly or the cluster is down.

**Fix:**
Run 'ibmcloud ks cluster-config --cluster my-cluster' to set up kubeconfig again.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient CPU resources'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the cluster does not have enough CPU resources available for the requested pods.

**Fix:**
Scale up your worker nodes or adjust the resource requests in your deployments.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'ImagePullBackOff'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the specified container image could not be pulled from the registry.

**Fix:**
Check the image name and ensure it is accessible from the cluster.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for PodSecurity admission, improved integration with IBM Cloud monitoring services.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated support for legacy Kubernetes versions 1.26 and below.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from 1.29, ensure that your workloads are compatible with the new PodSecurity standards.

resolution.summary: You should see the nodes listed with a 'Ready' status when running 'kubectl get nodes'.
codeSnippets: kubectl create clusterrolebinding cluster-admin-binding --clusterrole=cluster-admin --user=$(ibmcloud iam whoami)

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the IBM Cloud CLI and kubectl installed before proceeding.
